summary: FX Pro 2026 iteration 2 — new features (8 new currencies, /users/check, change-password, base64 profile picture, vault/coffre) plus full regression. 59/59 pytest cases passed (100%): 42 existing + 17 new.

minor.issue: After password change, old JWT tokens remain valid until natural expiry (7d). No session/token revocation. Consider rotating JWT secret per-user or maintaining a token version on the user doc.

Auto-status flip locked→ready is computed on the fly in the response only; not persisted. Acceptable, but downstream consumers querying directly from db.vaults will still see 'locked'. Document or persist.

Status check rejects only 'withdrawn'. A vault re-fetched immediately after a successful withdraw can race; recommend including status='locked' filter in the find_one or using $set on a conditional update.
picture field accepts any string (no size/MIME validation). A very large base64 payload could be stored. Recommend max-length and data:image/* prefix check.

action_items: Optional: invalidate active sessions on change-password (token versioning or session_id rotation).
Optional: validate `picture` payload (size + data URI prefix) in PATCH /api/profile.
Optional: persist vault status update from 'locked' to 'ready' (currently computed only in the GET list response).
Backend fully verified for iteration 2 — main agent may proceed to frontend implementation/testing.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now ~960 lines — exceeds the 700-line guideline. Strongly recommend splitting into modules (auth.py, rates.py, wallet.py, vault.py, profile.py, admin.py).
PATCH /api/profile still uses untyped Dict[str, Any]; add a Pydantic model with picture max-length and kyc_status enum validation.
users/check leaks `name`+`picture` of any registered email to any authenticated user — intentional for the UX (real-time validation) but consider rate-limiting to prevent email-enumeration scraping.
Vault penalty is hard-coded (5%). Recommend extracting to a constant or admin-configurable setting.

vault_withdraw uses two non-atomic updates (users.balances + vaults status); on failure mid-way, money could be credited without status change. Wrap in a transaction or implement idempotency via the vault doc.
All response models continue to exclude `_id` correctly. ✅
JWT_SECRET still 27 bytes — InsecureKeyLengthWarning persists (carry-over from iteration 1).
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/conftest.py (modified — added _ensure_demo_user to auto-recreate demo on password drift)
/app/backend/tests/test_backend_api.py (modified — root + rates checks now include 19 currencies)
seed_data_creation: TestChangePassword creates one TEST_pwd_* user per test method and cleans up via admin DELETE. Vault tests use the demo user (top-up via admin/balance to keep ≥100 EUR). Profile picture test restores picture=None after the assertion.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 59 backend endpoints verified (42 regression + 17 new). conftest.py now auto-recreates demo@fxpro.com via admin DELETE→register if its password drifted from Demo@2026 (this happens when frontend change-password flow is exercised between test runs). Pytest reusable at /app/backend/tests/. Live FX rates from open.er-api.com now include all 8 new currencies. Vault collection: db.vaults. Vault statuses: locked → ready (on-the-fly) → withdrawn. Early-withdraw penalty is 5%. /api/users/check returns {exists, name, email, picture} for others, {exists, self:true, name, email} for self, {exists:false} otherwise. Server.py is now 960+ lines and a refactor into modules is recommended.
rca_of_issue: No bugs found in iteration 2 endpoints. All happy-path and error-path responses match the spec from the review request.
